summary: This lecture discusses Islamic teachings about reliance on Allah (tawakkul) and trust in divine providence. The speaker uses the Quranic story of a man obsessed with his garden to illustrate the dangers of pride and self-reliance, contrasting this with the Islamic principle of placing one's trust completely in Allah. The sermon emphasizes that the opening verses of the Quran teach believers to seek Allah's help (bismillah) and praise Him (alhamdulillah) as fundamental lessons, rejecting contemporary secular philosophies of self-love and self-reliance. The speaker explains how Prophet Muhammad himself continuously asked Allah to manage his affairs and never trusted himself, serving as the ultimate example of tawakkul for Muslims.
suggested_title: The Quranic Lesson of Tawakkul: Trust in Allah Over Self-Reliance
